questions.q: What major milestone did the Artemis II mission, launched on April 1, 2026, achieve as it journeyed around the Moon?
options: Successfully landed humans on the lunar surface.
Established a permanent lunar base.
Broke the record for the farthest distance humans have traveled from Earth.
Discovered water ice at the lunar south pole.
questions.correctText: The Artemis II mission, launched on April 1, 2026, is expected to break the record for the farthest distance humans have travelled from Earth as it performs a lunar flyby.

questions.q: A recent large-scale study identified a "neural fingerprint" common to several psychedelic drugs. What is a key characteristic of this shared effect on the brain?
options: Decreased communication between brain networks.
Increased cross-talk between different brain systems.
Suppressed visual cortex activity only.
Reduced serotonin production.
questions.correctText: A new major study identified a common 'neural fingerprint' of psychedelic drugs, finding they increase cross-talk between different brain systems.

questions.q: According to a new study on human echolocation, what happens in the brains of expert blind echolocators as they use mouth clicks to perceive their surroundings?
options: The brain processes a single "optimal snapshot" of sound.
Neural activity related to object location weakens with more clicks.
Information about object location accumulates over successive clicks, building spatial representations.
Only visual areas of the brain are activated.
questions.correctText: New experimental EEG studies found that the accuracy of expert echolocators improved with each click, suggesting the brain accumulates acoustic evidence over time to build spatial representations.

questions.q: What significant geological activity was recently reported regarding Japan's Kikai Caldera, one of Earth's most explosive volcanoes?
options: It has completely run out of magma and is now dormant.
Its enormous magma chamber is slowly refilling.
A new island has formed directly above it.
It experienced a minor eruption that caused no damage.
questions.correctText: Researchers have revealed that Japan's Kikai Caldera, an underwater supervolcano, is slowly refilling with magma after its massive eruption 7,300 years ago.

questions.q: Scientists recently identified the gene SLC35F2, solving a 30-year mystery. What is the primary function of the nutrient, queuosine, that this gene helps transport into human cells?
options: It regulates blood sugar levels.
It strengthens bone density.
It is crucial for brain health, memory, stress response, and cancer defense.
It is responsible for muscle growth and repair.
questions.correctText: Scientists identified the gene SLC35F2, which is responsible for transporting queuosine, a rare micronutrient crucial for brain health, memory, stress response, and cancer defense, into human cells.
